title:
The Enigmatic Apsaras of Angkor Wat: Symbolism, Art, and Cultural Sign
– HD Asian Art
highlights: ethereal Apsaras of Angkor Wat are among the most captivating features of this iconic temple complex, gracing its walls with their celestial presence. These divine female figures, elegantly carved in sandstone, represent a blend of  ...    Angkor Wat, constructed in the early 12th century under King Suryavarman II, is famous for its intricate bas-reliefs and towering spires. Built as a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, it later became a center of Buddhist worship, reflecting Cambodia’s evolving religious landscape. The Apsaras at Angkor Wat are a testament to this cultural synthesis and represent a blend of Hindu and Cambodian influences.
